heading: Continuing jurisdiction and closing of the case
label: 945 Continuing jurisdiction and closing of the case
notes: senate report no. 95–989Section 948 [enacted as section 945] permits the court to retain jurisdiction over the case to ensure successful execution of the plan. The provision is the same as that found in section 96(e) of Chapter 9 of the present Act [section 416(e) of former title 11].

Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by effective with respect to cases filed 90 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 98–353section 552(a) of Pub. L. 98–353section 101 of this titleJuly 10, 1984
amendments: Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by effective with respect to cases filed 90 days after , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 98–353section 552(a) of Pub. L. 98–353section 101 of this titleJuly 10, 1984

children.label: a
children.text: The court may retain jurisdiction over the case for such period of time as is necessary for the successful implementation of the plan.

children.label: b
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (a) of this section, the court shall close the case when administration of the case has been completed.
